text.title: Testing Patterns (Cross-Language) Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed when testing-patterns is applied incorrectly. Each entry has a symptom (`description`), root cause (`reason`), and corrective fix (in `note`).

antipattern.description: Suite passes locally, fails in random order in CI.
antipattern.reason: Tests share mutable state through module-level cache or DB row.
antipattern.note: Fix: Move data setup to `beforeEach`/`afterEach`, destroy in reverse order; run with `--shuffle` locally.

antipattern.description: 100-line test where most lines build the input.
antipattern.reason: Inline literals copied 5+ times; small mutations to each.
antipattern.note: Fix: Extract a Builder with sensible defaults; tests call `userBuilder().withRole('admin').build()`.

antipattern.description: E2E suite is 80% of CI time but catches 5% of bugs.
antipattern.reason: Inverted pyramid: integration is missing, devs reached for E2E because it was the first thing they learned.
antipattern.note: Fix: Audit by layer; for each E2E that asserts business logic, write the equivalent integration test, then delete the E2E.

antipattern.description: Tests assert on private state via reflection.
antipattern.reason: Implementation-coupled assertions; refactor breaks the test even though behavior is unchanged.
antipattern.note: Fix: Assert on observable outputs only; if the only way to observe is via private state, the public API is incomplete.
